prevalence.trend: increasing due to rising NAFLD and alcohol-related cases

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: hepatitis C screening improving, NAFLD opportunistic

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: EASL Clinical Practice Guidelines, non-invasive fibrosis assessment (FibroScan, FIB-4)
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: DAAs for hepatitis C (elimination targets), NAFLD lifestyle + emerging therapies, alcohol programs, liver transplantation
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Rising NAFLD/NASH burden (25-30% adults), alcohol consumption (Eastern Europe), hepatitis elimination gaps
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: EASL guidelines, WHO hepatitis C elimination program, high transplant access, FibroScan availability
